---
title: "Coolbits causing problems with Low-Graphics mode"
date: 2008-07-06
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by thethirdmoose on 2008-07-06
Hi,
I built my new computer (E8400@3GHz, 8800GT, 2GB RAM, 8.04) about a week ago, and I'm trying to overclock my graphics card. I have the nvidia restricted drivers, so I added coolbits to my xorg.conf. Here's the file before:
```
# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder26)  Thu Feb 14 18:13:41 PST 2008

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Default Layout"
    Screen      0  "Default Screen" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "glx"
    Load           "v4l"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver         "kbd"
    Option         "XkbRules" "xorg"
    Option         "XkbModel" "pc105"
    Option         "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier     "Configured Mouse"
    Driver         "mouse"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier     "Configured Monitor"
    VendorName     "Generic LCD Display"
    ModelName      "LCD Panel 1680x1050"
    HorizSync       31.5 - 65.5
    VertRefresh     56.0 - 65.0
    Gamma           1
    ModeLine       "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -hsync -vsync
    ModeLine       "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -hsync -vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
    ModeLine       "1400x1050@60" 122.6 1400 1488 1640 1880 1050 1051 1054 1087 -hsync +vsync
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Configured Video Device"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    BoardName      "vesa"
    Screen          0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Default Screen"
    Device         "Configured Video Device"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection     "Display"
        Virtual     1680 1050
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1680x1050@60" "1024x768@60" "1280x960@60" "800x600@60" "1280x1024@60" "800x600@56" "1400x1050@60" "640x480@60"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

And after, I just put
```
Option "Coolbits" 1
```

at the bottom of the Device section and rebooted.

Not only did I not have the overclocking section in the nVidia control panel, the graphics system was shot and it put me in Low-Graphics mode, so I had to restore my xorg.conf. Is this a common problem? Am I doing something wrong?

---

### Post by rickdog on 2008-07-06
Have you tried using NVclock to overclock the graphics card? Currently it's what I use and it seems to work well.

---

### Post by thethirdmoose on 2008-07-06
I feel like such a fool. I did 
```
Option "Coolbits" 1
```
instead of
```
Option "Coolbits" "1"
```
Now it works. Thanks.

---
